notes.note: Caesar's grief in late 54 BC was for his daughter Julia, Pompey's wife, who had died in childbirth that August; the marriage had been the personal bond between the two dynasts. Cicero's tact in not writing to the bereaved Caesar dates this letter.

notes.anchor: especially now that Battara is dead
notes.note: An otherwise unknown man named Battara, whose recent death Cicero cites (with lost point) as removing any reason for Trebatius to hurry home. Probably a private joke between the two.

Terrae filius, literally 'son of the earth': a Roman idiom for a man of unknown or no family, a nobody. Cicero mocks Trebatius's social-climbing 'friend' by claiming not even to remember whether his name is Octavius or Cornelius.
